evidence.excerpt: slop-browser captures screenshots by downloading them to disk and discarding the data URL.

findings.description: Screenshot output required a file-system round trip and threw away the inline image payload, making visual inspection awkward for agents and forcing extra steps outside the CLI response.
findings.suggested_fix: Change the screenshot path so `slop screenshot` returns image data directly by default, with file saving as an optional flag instead of the only output mode.
findings.goal_alignment: Serves Fast and Agent-driven by letting the calling agent consume pixels immediately without extra IPC or disk work.

evidence.excerpt: Canvas elements are invisible to the agent.

findings.description: The agent could not inspect canvas-rendered UIs, which blocks automation on apps that render controls, previews, or state entirely inside canvas/WebGL.
findings.suggested_fix: Expose canvas discovery and read APIs from the content script, including bounds, dimensions, and pixel export via `toDataURL()` with optional WebGL `readPixels` support where available.
findings.goal_alignment: Serves Agent-driven and Undetectable by giving the external agent visual state without CDP.

findings.description: Only viewport capture was available, so agents could not inspect long pages or create a complete visual snapshot in one operation.
findings.suggested_fix: Implement full-page stitched capture using repeated viewport screenshots plus an offscreen document compositor, and return the stitched image directly to the caller.
findings.goal_alignment: Serves Undetectable and Fast by delivering full-page screenshots without CDP while reducing manual scroll-and-capture loops.

evidence.excerpt: Drag operations on canvas apps silently fail.

findings.description: Drag actions could report success while doing nothing on canvas-heavy apps, leaving the agent without a reliable way to manipulate visual editors or game-like UIs.
findings.suggested_fix: Add explicit coordinate-path drag execution with pointer down/move/up sequencing, verify post-action state, and surface a hard error when the target does not respond instead of failing silently.
findings.goal_alignment: Serves Resilient by making complex interactions observable and recoverable instead of silently broken.

evidence.excerpt: Session `ce33c009` exposed catastrophic inefficiency: an agent needed 368 CLI calls, 241 sleeps (209.7 seconds of blind waiting), 74 state re-queries, and 27 screenshots to place 8 shapes in Canva — an

findings.description: Complex workflows were far too chatty and slow, with huge command counts and large amounts of blind waiting just to complete a small canvas task.
findings.suggested_fix: Add batch execution, persistent command sessions, cached state reuse, and multi-action round trips so a workflow can issue fewer higher-value commands.
findings.goal_alignment: Directly serves Fast by collapsing IPC overhead and eliminating most of the avoidable wait time.

evidence.excerpt: replace blind sleeps with event-driven readiness signals, and make element targeting resilient to SPA DOM mutations

findings.description: The agent was compensating for missing readiness and stale-target handling with manual sleeps and repeated re-targeting, which is brittle on SPAs and dynamic editors.
findings.suggested_fix: Add DOM-stability and navigation-readiness primitives plus stable element refs/versioning so agents can wait on real signals instead of retry loops.
findings.goal_alignment: Serves Resilient and Fast by reducing stale-DOM failures and replacing polling-style workarounds with deterministic readiness checks.
